title: ​The 2026 Red Sox’ run prevention approach has been a disaster out of the gate
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Red Sox are off to a 1-5 start, their worst six-game opening since 2019, after being swept by the Astros.
Boston's starting rotation, including Garcia, Suarez, and Oviedo, has struggled with a collective 5.22 ERA and 11 earned runs.
The Red Sox committed six errors in six games, continuing defensive issues from last season where they led the league in errors.

title: Stefon Diggs has trial date set in strangulation, assault case
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Stefon Diggs's trial for strangulation and assault is set to begin on May 4 in Dedham District Court.
Diggs, a former Patriots wide receiver, pleaded not guilty to charges from a December 2 incident with his personal chef.
The New England Patriots expressed support for Diggs, denying the allegations as a financial dispute with an employee.

title: ‘What we had talked about, take that to the grave’: Report reveals details about State Police sergeant who allegedly drove drunk, caused fatal crash
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Massachusetts State Police sergeant Scott Quigley was indicted for negligent motor vehicle homicide while driving under influence in a 2023 crash.
Middlesex DA's office claims they were not officially notified about Quigley's involvement in the fatal crash until recent months.
State Police and local reports omitted crucial details about the crash, including Quigley's alleged intoxication and the van's injuries.

title: Dorchester woman sentenced for fatal stabbing stemming from online feud
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Alyssa Partsch, 33, from Dorchester, sentenced to 15-20 years for manslaughter in 2023 fatal stabbing of Jazreanna Sheppard.
Partsch and Sheppard had an online feud before the stabbing at Park Street MBTA station in Boston.
Partsch previously pleaded guilty to a violent crime in 2017; no comment from her attorneys on the new sentence.

title: NBA superstar’s message to Connecticut Sun fans: ‘They’ll be alright, they’ve got UConn’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Connecticut Sun WNBA team is relocating to Houston, rebranding as the Houston Comets.
NBA star Kevin Durant expressed support for the relocation while acknowledging Connecticut's loss, pointing to UConn women's basketball as a consolation.
A $325 million bid to move the Sun to Boston was blocked, with Houston securing a $300 million record bid instead.

title: Alex Cora declines to elaborate after scratching Carlos Narvaez hours before Red Sox game
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox manager Alex Cora scratched Carlos Narvaez from the lineup hours before the game against the Houston Astros.
Cora declined to provide specifics to reporters about the decision, saying it was the best choice for the team.
Narvaez, batting .444 this season, and Connor Wong, who replaced him, both refrained from commenting on the reasons.

title: Mike Vrabel says Patriots will pick up Christian Gonzalez’s fifth-year option
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots plan to pick up Christian Gonzalez's fifth-year option, keeping him with the team through 2027.
Gonzalez's option for 2027 is valued at $18.1 million, a significant increase from his previous salary.
Gonzalez, a 2023 first-round pick, earned his first Pro Bowl nod despite injury concerns and limited play in 2026.

title: Elias Lindholm, like the Bruins, is getting hot at the ideal time
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Elias Lindholm has scored five goals and two assists in his last six games for the Bruins.
The Bruins have won four straight games, solidifying a likely playoff position this season.
David Pastrnak leads with a career-best 66 assists, contributing significantly to the Bruins' offensive strength.

title: For this Everett runner, the Boston Marathon has always been personal
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Michelle Davidson is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Brigham Stepping Strong Foundation, supporting trauma care.
Davidson's motivation stems from the impact of the 2013 Boston Marathon bombings and the city's resilience.
A family from Davidson's community was directly affected by the bombings, making her connection to the marathon personal.

title: Boston faces budget deficit of nearly $50 million
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston faces a $48.4 million budget deficit, primarily due to snow removal, police overtime, and health insurance costs.
Chief Financial Officer Ashley Groffenberger outlined efforts to delay hiring and freeze some spending to address the deficit.
Boston Public Schools are dealing with a separate $53 million deficit, resulting in a partial hiring freeze and proposed cuts.
